[2026-06-09T22:51:56.057Z] [INFO]             "type": "text",
[2026-06-09T22:51:56.057Z] [INFO]             "text": "I have all the facts needed. Here is the structured report.\n\n---\n\n# Research Report: models.dev for a Client-Side SLM Catalog\n\n## 1. What it is, who maintains it, repository\n\n**models.dev** is \"a comprehensive open-source database of AI model specifications, pricing, and capabilities.\" It exists because there is no single database of all available AI models. It is a community-contributed project that is also used internally by **opencode** (the open-source AI coding agent).\n\n- **Maintainer / org:** the team behind **SST** and **opencode** (sst org on GitHub). The site footer credits opencode; the repo lives under the `sst` org.\n- **GitHub repo:** `https://github.com/sst/models.dev` (~4.9k stars, ~1.1k forks at time of research).\n- **Website:** `https://models.dev`\n- It serves both the interactive web UI and a public JSON API, generated from TOML source files, deployed on Cloudflare's edge.\n\n## 2. How the catalog data is structured\n\n**Format: per-model TOML files**, validated by Zod schemas (`packages/core/src/schema.ts`). Monorepo with `packages/` (core, web, function).\n\n**Two-layer data model:**\n\n- **Provider-agnostic model metadata** lives in `models/<provider>/<model-id>.toml` (209 files in the clone). Example: `models/openai/gpt-5.toml`, `models/alibaba/qwen3-32b.toml`. Facts about the model itself, independent of who serves it.\n- **Provider-specific serving details** live in `providers/<provider>/models/<model-id>.toml` (141 provider folders, ~4,000 provider-model files). Example: `providers/openai/models/gpt-5.toml`. These carry pricing, provider context limits, request shape, etc.\n- Each provider has `providers/<provider>/provider.toml` plus an optional `logo.svg`.\n- Model IDs with a `/` use subfolders (e.g. ID `openai/gpt-5` → folder `openai/`, file `gpt-5.toml`).\n- **`id` is auto-injected from the filename** — never put `id` in the TOML.\n- Provider TOMLs can inherit model metadata via `base_model = \"<provider>/<model-id>\"` and then override only provider-specific fields; `base_model_omit = [\"limit.input\"]` removes inherited fields. Schemas are `.strict()` — extra/unknown fields fail validation.\n\n**Model metadata fields** (from the Zod `ModelMetadata` / `Model` schemas and README):\n\n- Identity: `id` (auto), `name`, `family` (enum, e.g. `qwen`, `gpt`, `deepseek-thinking`)\n- Dates: `release_date`, `last_updated`, `knowledge` (cutoff) — all `YYYY-MM` or `YYYY-MM-DD`\n- Capability flags (booleans): `attachment`, `reasoning`, `tool_call`, `structured_output`, `temperature`\n- Reasoning detail: `reasoning_options` (toggle / effort with values `none|minimal|low|medium|high|xhigh|max` / `budget_tokens` with min/max), `interleaved`\n- **`open_weights`** (boolean) — whether trained weights are publicly available\n- **`license`** (string), **`weights`** (array), **`links`** (array)\n- `modalities`: `{ input: [...], output: [...] }` where each is from `text | audio | image | video | pdf`\n- `limit`: `{ context, input?, output? }` (token counts)\n- `cost` (provider TOMLs): `input`, `output` (per **million** tokens, USD), plus optional `reasoning`, `cache_read`, `cache_write`, `input_audio`, `output_audio`, and context-based `tiers` / `context_over_200k`\n- `status`: `alpha | beta | deprecated`\n- `benchmarks`: array of `{ name, score, metric?, harness?, variant?, dataset?, version?, source?, date? }`\n\n**`weights` array entry schema** (key for an on-device app — points to downloadable weights):\n```toml\n[[weights]]\nlabel = \"Hugging Face\"\nurl = \"https://huggingface.co/Qwen/Qwen3-32B\"\nformat = \"safetensors\"      # optional\nquantization = \"Q4_K_M\"     # optional (schema supports it; rarely populated currently)\n```\n\n**`links` types:** `announcement | blog | docs | license | model_card | paper | weights | other`.\n\n## 3. The API\n\nThree endpoints (README + live verification):\n\n- **`https://models.dev/api.json`** — full provider catalog. Top-level object **keyed by provider id**; each provider has `id`, `name`, `env` (auth env var names), `npm` (AI SDK package), `api` (base URL, for openai-compatible), `doc`, and `models` (object keyed by model id). Model entries include the merged capability flags, `modalities`, `open_weights`, `limit`, and `cost`.\n- **`https://models.dev/models.json`** — provider-agnostic model metadata, **keyed by model id** (e.g. `alibaba/qwen3-32b`). This is the right endpoint for an on-device catalog: it carries `open_weights`, `weights[]` (HF URLs), `license`, `benchmarks`, `modalities`, `limit` — without provider pricing noise.\n- **`https://models.dev/catalog.json`** — both combined in one response.\n- **Logos:** `https://models.dev/logos/{provider}.svg` and `/logos/labs/{lab}.svg` (currentColor SVGs; default logo if missing).\n\nThe Model ID is the AI SDK identifier, intended for direct lookups.\n\n> Note: the `models.json` file checked into the cloned repo is a different (OpenRouter-style) data dump with fields like `canonical_slug`/`hugging_face_id`; it does **not** match the live `https://models.dev/models.json` shape. Treat the **live endpoint** and the **TOML source files** as authoritative, not the repo's committed `models.json`.\n\n### Example single model entry (live `models.json`)\n```json\n{\n  \"alibaba/qwen3-32b\": {\n    \"id\": \"alibaba/qwen3-32b\",\n    \"name\": \"Qwen3 32B\",\n    \"family\": \"qwen\",\n    \"attachment\": false,\n    \"reasoning\": true,\n    \"tool_call\": true,\n    \"temperature\": true,\n    \"knowledge\": \"2025-04\",\n    \"release_date\": \"2025-04\",\n    \"last_updated\": \"2025-04\",\n    \"modalities\": { \"input\": [\"text\"], \"output\": [\"text\"] },\n    \"open_weights\": true,\n    \"limit\": { \"context\": 131072, \"output\": 16384 },\n    \"weights\": [\n      { \"label\": \"Hugging Face\", \"url\": \"https://huggingface.co/Qwen/Qwen3-32B\" }\n    ],\n    \"benchmarks\": [\n      { \"name\": \"Aider Polyglot\", \"score\": 40, \"metric\": \"percent correct\",\n        \"source\": \"https://aider.chat/docs/leaderboards/\", \"date\": \"2025-05-08\" }\n    ]\n  }\n}\n```\n\n## 4. License\n\n**MIT License** (`LICENSE`, \"Copyright (c) 2025 models.dev\"). You can reuse, redistribute, and modify the data and code freely with attribution and license inclusion. Good fit for bundling the catalog into a browser app.\n\n## 5. Open-weights coverage and how to filter/extend\n\nIt covers **both** hosted/API models and open-weights models — it is not local-first, but open-weights is a first-class concept:\n\n- Boolean **`open_weights`** flag on every model.\n- **`weights[]`** array with `url` (usually a Hugging Face repo), optional `format` and `quantization`.\n- Optional `license` and `links` (incl. `model_card`, `weights`).\n\n**Counts in the cloned source TOMLs:**\n- `models/` (provider-agnostic): **91 open-weights** vs 118 closed (209 total).\n- `providers/` (provider-model serving files): ~1,490 open-weights vs ~2,526 closed.\n- ~70 model-metadata files carry a `[[weights]]` block with download URLs.\n\n**How to filter for on-device:** fetch `models.json`, keep entries where `open_weights === true` **and** `weights[].url` exists, then narrow by your runtime constraints — small `limit.context`, parameter count parsed from the name/family (e.g. ≤3-4B for WebGPU/wasm), and `modalities.input === [\"text\"]`.\n\n**Gaps to extend yourself** (models.dev does not provide these, and you'll need them for a browser app):\n- No **parameter count**, **file size on disk**, or **RAM/VRAM** estimates.\n- No **GGUF / ONNX / MLC / web-specific quantization** artifacts — `weights[].format`/`quantization` exist in the schema but are largely unpopulated, and entries point to base HF repos, not browser-ready builds (e.g. transformers.js ONNX, MLC-LLM WASM, or web-llm prebuilt model libraries).\n- No **download/runtime compatibility** info (WebGPU vs WASM, context-length-vs-memory).\n\nPractical approach: use models.dev (MIT, so embeddable) as the canonical metadata/pricing/benchmark layer keyed by model id, then **join** it to a Hugging Face query (or to web-llm / transformers.js prebuilt model lists) to resolve actual browser-runnable quantized artifacts and sizes. Because the data is MIT TOML, you could also fork/extend it by adding `weights` entries with `format`/`quantization` for your runtime, or maintain an overlay file keyed by the same model ids.\n\n## 6. Similar catalogs / registries\n\n- **Hugging Face Hub** — the canonical source of open-weights artifacts. `huggingface.co/models`, with a public API (`huggingface_hub` lib, `/api/models` with filter/search params and per-repo `model_card`/`config.json` metadata). This is where models.dev's `weights[].url` point. Best source for actual downloadable files, sizes, and quantizations (GGUF/ONNX/safetensors). `https://huggingface.co/models`\n- **Artificial Analysis** — leaderboard benchmarking >100 serverless LLM endpoints on price/speed/quality (open + proprietary); HF Space + site. Strong on measured throughput/TTFT and quality, weaker as a structured embeddable catalog. `https://artificialanalysis.ai` / `https://huggingface.co/spaces/ArtificialAnalysis/LLM-Performance-Leaderboard`\n- **LLM-Stats** (`llm-stats.com`) — tracks ~309 canonical models across labs/providers; capability, price, speed, context length; daily-updated open-source community data; has an Open LLM Leaderboard view. `https://llm-stats.com`\n- **Open LLM Leaderboard** (Hugging Face) — academic benchmark rankings for open models. `https://huggingface.co/spaces/open-llm-leaderboard/open_llm_leaderboard`\n- **simonw/llm model registry** (`github.com/simonw/llm`) — Python/CLI model registry where plugins register models via entry points. For **local/on-device** specifically, relevant plugins: `llm-gguf` (llama.cpp/GGUF), `llm-mlx` (Apple MLX), `llm-ollama`, `llm-llamafile`, `llm-mlc` (MLC), `llm-gpt4all`. The registry is plugin-driven (no single embeddable JSON catalog), but the plugin directory is a good map of local runtimes. `https://llm.datasette.io/en/stable/plugins/directory.html`\n- For a **browser** app specifically, also look at runtime-bundled catalogs not surfaced above: **web-llm** prebuilt model list and **transformers.js** (ONNX) models on HF — these are the actual sources of browser-runnable quantized weights you'll join models.dev metadata against.\n\n---\n\n## Sources\n\n- https://models.dev/\n- https://github.com/sst/models.dev\n- https://models.dev/api.json\n- https://models.dev/models.json\n- https://models.dev/catalog.json (referenced in README)\n- README.md, AGENTS.md, sync.md and `packages/core/src/schema.ts` in `github.com/sst/models.dev` (inspected via local clone)\n- https://deepwiki.com/sst/models.dev\n- https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48241172\n- https://opencode.ai\n- https://huggingface.co/models\n- https://artificialanalysis.ai\n- https://huggingface.co/spaces/ArtificialAnalysis/LLM-Performance-Leaderboard\n- https://huggingface.co/blog/leaderboard-artificial-analysis\n- https://llm-stats.com\n- https://llm-stats.com/leaderboards/open-llm-leaderboard\n- https://huggingface.co/spaces/open-llm-leaderboard/open_llm_leaderboard\n- https://github.com/simonw/llm\n- https://llm.datasette.io/en/stable/plugins/directory.html\n- https://github.com/simonw/llm-mlc\n- https://github.com/simonw/llm-lm-studio\n\n**Key takeaway for your app:** Use `https://models.dev/models.json` (MIT-licensed, model-id-keyed) as your metadata/benchmark/pricing layer, filter on `open_weights === true` + presence of `weights[].url`, and join to Hugging Face / web-llm / transformers.js to resolve actual browser-runnable quantized artifacts and on-disk sizes — which models.dev does not currently provide. The most relevant files in the repo are `packages/core/src/schema.ts` (authoritative field definitions), `README.md`, and the `models/` + `providers/` TOML trees."

This is the right endpoint for an on-device catalog: it carries `open_weights`, `weights[]` (HF URLs), `license`, `benchmarks`, `modalities`, `limit` — without provider pricing noise.\n- **`https://models.dev/catalog.json`** — both combined in one response.\n- **Logos:** `https://models.dev/logos/{provider}.svg` and `/logos/labs/{lab}.svg` (currentColor SVGs; default logo if missing).\n\nThe Model ID is the AI SDK identifier, intended for direct lookups.\n\n> Note: the `models.json` file checked into the cloned repo is a different (OpenRouter-style) data dump with fields like `canonical_slug`/`hugging_face_id`; it does **not** match the live `https://models.dev/models.json` shape. Treat the **live endpoint** and the **TOML source files** as authoritative, not the repo's committed `models.json`.\n\n### Example single model entry (live `models.json`)\n```json\n{\n  \"alibaba/qwen3-32b\": {\n    \"id\": \"alibaba/qwen3-32b\",\n    \"name\": \"Qwen3 32B\",\n    \"family\": \"qwen\",\n    \"attachment\": false,\n    \"reasoning\": true,\n    \"tool_call\": true,\n    \"temperature\": true,\n    \"knowledge\": \"2025-04\",\n    \"release_date\": \"2025-04\",\n    \"last_updated\": \"2025-04\",\n    \"modalities\": { \"input\": [\"text\"], \"output\": [\"text\"] },\n    \"open_weights\": true,\n    \"limit\": { \"context\": 131072, \"output\": 16384 },\n    \"weights\": [\n      { \"label\": \"Hugging Face\", \"url\": \"https://huggingface.co/Qwen/Qwen3-32B\" }\n    ],\n    \"benchmarks\": [\n      { \"name\": \"Aider Polyglot\", \"score\": 40, \"metric\": \"percent correct\",\n        \"source\": \"https://aider.chat/docs/leaderboards/\", \"date\": \"2025-05-08\" }\n    ]\n  }\n}\n```\n\n## 4. License\n\n**MIT License** (`LICENSE`, \"Copyright (c) 2025 models.dev\"). You can reuse, redistribute, and modify the data and code freely with attribution and license inclusion. Good fit for bundling the catalog into a browser app.\n\n## 5. Open-weights coverage and how to filter/extend\n\nIt covers **both** hosted/API models and open-weights models — it is not local-first, but open-weights is a first-class concept:\n\n- Boolean **`open_weights`** flag on every model.\n- **`weights[]`** array with `url` (usually a Hugging Face repo), optional `format` and `quantization`.\n- Optional `license` and `links` (incl. `model_card`, `weights`).\n\n**Counts in the cloned source TOMLs:**\n- `models/` (provider-agnostic): **91 open-weights** vs 118 closed (209 total).\n- `providers/` (provider-model serving files): ~1,490 open-weights vs ~2,526 closed.\n- ~70 model-metadata files carry a `[[weights]]` block with download URLs.\n\n**How to filter for on-device:** fetch `models.json`, keep entries where `open_weights === true` **and** `weights[].url` exists, then narrow by your runtime constraints — small `limit.context`, parameter count parsed from the name/family (e.g. ≤3-4B for WebGPU/wasm), and `modalities.input === [\"text\"]`.\n\n**Gaps to extend yourself** (models.dev does not provide these, and you'll need them for a browser app):\n- No **parameter count**, **file size on disk**, or **RAM/VRAM** estimates.\n- No **GGUF / ONNX / MLC / web-specific quantization** artifacts — `weights[].format`/`quantization` exist in the schema but are largely unpopulated, and entries point to base HF repos, not browser-ready builds (e.g. transformers.js ONNX, MLC-LLM WASM, or web-llm prebuilt model libraries).\n- No **download/runtime compatibility** info (WebGPU vs WASM, context-length-vs-memory).\n\nPractical approach: use models.dev (MIT, so embeddable) as the canonical metadata/pricing/benchmark layer keyed by model id, then **join** it to a Hugging Face query (or to web-llm / transformers.js prebuilt model lists) to resolve actual browser-runnable quantized artifacts and sizes. Because the data is MIT TOML, you could also fork/extend it by adding `weights` entries with `format`/`quantization` for your runtime, or maintain an overlay file keyed by the same model ids.\n\n## 6. Similar catalogs / registries\n\n- **Hugging Face Hub** — the canonical source of open-weights artifacts. `huggingface.co/models`, with a public API (`huggingface_hub` lib, `/api/models` with filter/search params and per-repo `model_card`/`config.json` metadata). This is where models.dev's `weights[].url` point. Best source for actual downloadable files, sizes, and quantizations (GGUF/ONNX/safetensors). `https://huggingface.co/models`\n- **Artificial Analysis** — leaderboard benchmarking >100 serverless LLM endpoints on price/speed/quality (open + proprietary); HF Space + site. Strong on measured throughput/TTFT and quality, weaker as a structured embeddable catalog. `https://artificialanalysis.ai` / `https://huggingface.co/spaces/ArtificialAnalysis/LLM-Performance-Leaderboard`\n- **LLM-Stats** (`llm-stats.com`) — tracks ~309 canonical models across labs/providers; capability, price, speed, context length; daily-updated open-source community data; has an Open LLM Leaderboard view. `https://llm-stats.com`\n- **Open LLM Leaderboard** (Hugging Face) — academic benchmark rankings for open models. `https://huggingface.co/spaces/open-llm-leaderboard/open_llm_leaderboard`\n- **simonw/llm model registry** (`github.com/simonw/llm`) — Python/CLI model registry where plugins register models via entry points. For **local/on-device** specifically, relevant plugins: `llm-gguf` (llama.cpp/GGUF), `llm-mlx` (Apple MLX), `llm-ollama`, `llm-llamafile`, `llm-mlc` (MLC), `llm-gpt4all`. The registry is plugin-driven (no single embeddable JSON catalog), but the plugin directory is a good map of local runtimes. `https://llm.datasette.io/en/stable/plugins/directory.html`\n- For a **browser** app specifically, also look at runtime-bundled catalogs not surfaced above: **web-llm** prebuilt model list and **transformers.js** (ONNX) models on HF — these are the actual sources of browser-runnable quantized weights you'll join models.dev metadata against.\n\n---\n\n## Sources\n\n- https://models.dev/\n- https://github.com/sst/models.dev\n- https://models.dev/api.json\n- https://models.dev/models.json\n- https://models.dev/catalog.json (referenced in README)\n- README.md, AGENTS.md, sync.md and `packages/core/src/schema.ts` in `github.com/sst/models.dev` (inspected via local clone)\n- https://deepwiki.com/sst/models.dev\n- https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48241172\n- https://opencode.ai\n- https://huggingface.co/models\n- https://artificialanalysis.ai\n- https://huggingface.co/spaces/ArtificialAnalysis/LLM-Performance-Leaderboard\n- https://huggingface.co/blog/leaderboard-artificial-analysis\n- https://llm-stats.com\n- https://llm-stats.com/leaderboards/open-llm-leaderboard\n- https://huggingface.co/spaces/open-llm-leaderboard/open_llm_leaderboard\n- https://github.com/simonw/llm\n- https://llm.datasette.io/en/stable/plugins/directory.html\n- https://github.com/simonw/llm-mlc\n- https://github.com/simonw/llm-lm-studio\n\n**Key takeaway for your app:** Use `https://models.dev/models.json` (MIT-licensed, model-id-keyed) as your metadata/benchmark/pricing layer, filter on `open_weights === true` + presence of `weights[].url`, and join to Hugging Face / web-llm / transformers.js to resolve actual browser-runnable quantized artifacts and on-disk sizes — which models.dev does not currently provide. The most relevant files in the repo are `packages/core/src/schema.ts` (authoritative field definitions), `README.md`, and the `models/` + `providers/` TOML trees."

[2026-06-09T22:52:17.307Z] [INFO]             "text": "I now have comprehensive, empirically-verified data. I have everything needed for the report. Let me compile the final structured markdown report.\n\n---\n\n# Hugging Face Hub API: Discovering & Downloading Small Models for Browser/Client-Side LLMs\n\nAll endpoint behaviors below were **verified empirically** with live `curl` calls against `huggingface.co` on 2026-06-09 (anonymous, no token), in addition to the official docs. The official Hub API reference has been migrated to an OpenAPI spec (`https://huggingface.co/.well-known/openapi.json` / `.md`) and an [OpenAPI Playground](https://huggingface.co/spaces/huggingface/openapi).\n\n## 1. `GET https://huggingface.co/api/models` — discovery & ranking\n\n### Query parameters (all confirmed working anonymously)\n\n| Param | Purpose | Example value |\n|---|---|---|\n| `search` | Free-text match on model id/name | `search=SmolLM2` |\n| `author` | Filter by namespace/org/user | `author=onnx-community` |\n| `filter` | Filter by **tag** (repeatable; AND-combined). Tags include library names, formats, languages, licenses | `filter=gguf`, `filter=transformers.js`, `filter=onnx` |\n| `pipeline_tag` | Filter by task | `pipeline_tag=text-generation` |\n| `library` | Filter by library | `library=transformers`, `library=transformers.js` |\n| `language` | Language code | `language=en` |\n| `sort` | Sort key: `downloads`, `likes`, `trendingScore`, `createdAt`, `lastModified` | `sort=downloads` |\n| `direction` | `-1` = descending, `1` = ascending | `direction=-1` |\n| `limit` | Page size (cap results) | `limit=20` |\n| `full` | `full=true` adds `tags`, `siblings`, `sha`, etc. to each entry | `full=true` |\n| `config` | `config=true` includes the model `config.json` summary | `config=true` |\n| `expand[]` | Selectively expand fields (alternative to `full`); e.g. `siblings`, `safetensors`, `gguf`, `downloads`, `likes`, `tags`, `pipeline_tag` | `expand[]=safetensors` |\n| `gated` | Filter gated repos | `gated=false` |\n\n> Note: `expand[]` and `full`/`config` are mutually exclusive in practice — use one style. With `expand[]`, only the requested fields (plus id) are returned.\n\n### Example URLs\n\n```\n# Top-downloaded SmolLM2 models, full metadata\nhttps://huggingface.co/api/models?search=SmolLM2&sort=downloads&direction=-1&limit=10&full=true\n\n# Trending text-generation models\nhttps://huggingface.co/api/models?pipeline_tag=text-generation&sort=trendingScore&direction=-1&limit=20\n\n# Browser-runnable (transformers.js) text-gen models by popularity\nhttps://huggingface.co/api/models?filter=transformers.js&pipeline_tag=text-generation&sort=downloads&limit=20\n\n# Most-liked ONNX exports from onnx-community\nhttps://huggingface.co/api/models?author=onnx-community&pipeline_tag=text-generation&sort=downloads&limit=20\n\n# All GGUF repos, most downloaded\nhttps://huggingface.co/api/models?filter=gguf&sort=downloads&limit=20\n```\n\n### Example response shape (one element, `sort=downloads`)\n\n```json\n{\n  \"id\": \"HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM2-135M-Instruct\",\n  \"author\": \"HuggingFaceTB\",\n  \"gated\": false,\n  \"likes\": 340,\n  \"downloads\": 1543897,\n  \"trendingScore\": 270,\n  \"private\": false,\n  \"tags\": [\"transformers\",\"onnx\",\"safetensors\",\"llama\",\"text-generation\",\n           \"transformers.js\",\"conversational\",\"en\",\"license:apache-2.0\", \"...\"],\n  \"pipeline_tag\": \"text-generation\",\n  \"library_name\": \"transformers\",\n  \"createdAt\": \"2024-10-31T13:41:10.000Z\",\n  \"siblings\": [{\"rfilename\": \"model.safetensors\"}, {\"rfilename\": \"onnx/model_q4.onnx\"}, \"...\"]\n}\n```\n\n**Pagination:** results page via the `Link` HTTP header (`rel=\"next\"`) and `X-Total-Count` (both are CORS-exposed — see §3).\n\n---\n\n## 2. Per-file sizes & parameter counts\n\n### Tree endpoint — the reliable way to get file sizes\n\n`GET https://huggingface.co/api/models/{repo}/tree/{revision}?recursive=true`\n\nParams: `recursive=true` (walk subdirs), `expand=true` (adds commit + security-scan data), `limit`, `cursor`. You can also scope to a subpath: `.../tree/main/onnx?recursive=true`.\n\nExample: `https://huggingface.co/api/models/HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM2-135M-Instruct/tree/main?recursive=true`\n\n```json\n[\n  {\"type\":\"directory\",\"oid\":\"703e76c4...\",\"size\":0,\"path\":\"onnx\"},\n  {\"type\":\"file\",\"oid\":\"2ef70737...\",\"size\":269060552,\n   \"lfs\":{\"oid\":\"5af571cb...\",\"size\":269060552,\"pointerSize\":134},\n   \"xetHash\":\"73b079dc...\",\"path\":\"model.safetensors\"},\n  {\"type\":\"file\",\"size\":137147867, \"lfs\":{\"size\":137147867,...},\n   \"path\":\"onnx/model_int8.onnx\"}\n]\n```\n\n- `size` is the **real byte size** (bytes). For LFS/Xet-backed files, `size` and `lfs.size` match the actual download size.\n- **Caveat:** sharded ONNX models store weights in external `model_xxx.onnx_data` files — the `.onnx` graph file then reports a tiny size (often `0`/few KB) and you must **sum the `.onnx` + its `.onnx_data*` siblings** to get true size (e.g. `onnx-community/Llama-3.2-1B-Instruct-ONNX`: `model_q4.onnx`≈0 MB + `model_q4.onnx_data`≈1693 MB).\n\n### `siblings` does NOT include sizes\n\nThe model-info `siblings` array (from `?full=true` or `?expand[]=siblings`) contains **only filenames**:\n```json\n\"siblings\": [{\"rfilename\": \".gitattributes\"}, {\"rfilename\": \"model.safetensors\"}]\n```\nSo for sizing you must use the **tree endpoint**, not `siblings`. (`?blobs=true` is a UI/blob-oriented flag; the tree endpoint's `size`/`lfs.size` is the dependable size source.)\n\n### Total parameter count — `?expand[]=safetensors`\n\n`GET https://huggingface.co/api/models/{repo}?expand[]=safetensors`\n\n```json\n{\n  \"id\": \"HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM2-135M-Instruct\",\n  \"safetensors\": {\n    \"parameters\": {\"BF16\": 134515008},\n    \"total\": 134515008\n  }\n}\n```\n`parameters` is a map of dtype → count; `total` is the grand total. You can request both `expand[]=safetensors&expand[]=gguf`. Under the hood the Hub derives this by range-reading the safetensors header / `model.safetensors.index.json` (the `huggingface.js` `parseSafetensorsMetadata` does the same, returning `parameterCount` and `parameterTotal`).\n\n---\n\n## 3. Download (resolve) URL pattern + CORS\n\n### Pattern\n```\nhttps://huggingface.co/{repo}/resolve/{revision}/{path}\ne.g. https://huggingface.co/HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM2-135M-Instruct/resolve/main/onnx/model_q4.onnx\n```\n`{revision}` = branch (`main`), tag, or commit SHA. Use a commit SHA for cache-immutable URLs.\n\n### CORS behavior (verified — browser fetch friendly)\n- A `GET` to `/resolve/...` returns **HTTP 302** redirecting to the Xet CDN (`https://cas-bridge.xethub.hf.co/...`) with a signed, time-limited URL. `fetch()` follows this transparently.\n- The huggingface.co response includes `access-control-allow-origin: <echoes your Origin>` and `access-control-expose-headers: ... Content-Range, ETag, X-Linked-Size, X-Linked-ETag, X-Xet-Hash, Accept-Ranges, ...`. `X-Linked-Size` gives the file size on the redirect response.\n- **Range requests work cross-origin.** A `Range: bytes=0-1023` request returns **206 Partial Content** from the CDN with `access-control-allow-origin`, `access-control-allow-credentials: true`, and exposed `content-range`/`accept-ranges`. \n- **CORS preflight (OPTIONS) succeeds:** `OPTIONS` with `Access-Control-Request-Headers: range` → **200** with `access-control-allow-methods: GET`, `access-control-allow-headers: range`, `access-control-max-age: 86400`.\n\n> This means streaming/chunked/resumable downloads from the browser (as transformers.js, WebLLM, and wllama do) work without a proxy. (Historical note: some earlier GitHub issues reported Range/preflight CORS failures on the Xet bridge for *dataset* Parquet partial reads — but model `/resolve/` Range fetches are working as tested here.)\n\n---\n\n## 4. Filtering for browser-runnable formats\n\n| Runtime | What to filter | Orgs / tags |\n|---|---|---|\n| **transformers.js** (ONNX Runtime Web, WebGPU) | `filter=transformers.js` or `library=transformers.js`; tag `onnx` | **onnx-community** (recommended, current exports), **Xenova** (legacy v2 ONNX). UI filter: `https://huggingface.co/models?library=transformers.js` |\n| **WebLLM / MLC** | `author=mlc-ai`; repos suffixed with quant scheme e.g. `-q4f16_1-MLC`, `-q4f32_1-MLC` | **mlc-ai** org (prebuilt WASM/WebGPU libraries). Also `mlc-llm` tag |\n| **wllama / llama.cpp-wasm** | `filter=gguf`; tag `gguf` | `bartowski`, `TheBloke`, `Qwen`, `microsoft`, `unsloth`, etc. |\n\nUseful task/format tags: `text-generation` (pipeline_tag), `transformers.js`, `onnx`, `gguf`, `conversational`, `mlc-llm`. ONNX repos expose multiple precisions as separate files in `onnx/`: `model.onnx` (fp32), `model_fp16.onnx`, `model_int8.onnx`/`model_quantized.onnx`/`model_uint8.onnx`, `model_q4.onnx`, `model_q4f16.onnx`, `model_bnb4.onnx` — letting the app pick a precision by device budget.\n\n> Note: transformers.js currently runs **ONNX only** (no GGUF). GGUF in-browser needs wllama/llama.cpp-wasm; MLC format needs WebLLM.\n\n---\n\n## 5. Quantization → size (verified file sizes, MB ≈ size/1e6)\n\nONNX dtypes: `fp16` ≈ 2 bytes/param, `int8`/`q8`/`uint8` ≈ 1 byte/param, `q4`/`q4f16` ≈ 0.5–0.6 byte/param. GGUF: `Q4_K_M` ≈ 4.5 bits/param, `Q8_0` ≈ 8.5 bits/param, `f16` ≈ 16 bits/param.\n\n### ONNX (transformers.js) — measured\n\n| Model | fp16 | int8/quantized | q4 | q4f16 |\n|---|---|---|---|---|\n| SmolLM2-135M-Instruct | 270 MB | 137 MB | — | — (fp32 `model.onnx` = 540 MB; safetensors = 269 MB) |\n| SmolLM2-360M-Instruct | — | — | — | — (fp32 = 1450 MB; safetensors = 724 MB) |\n| onnx-community/Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct | 997 MB | 512 MB | 786 MB | **483 MB** |\n| onnx-community/Llama-3.2-1B-Instruct (sharded, sum `.onnx`+`.onnx_data`) | ~2488 MB | 1237 MB | ~1693 MB | ~1090 MB |\n\n### GGUF (wllama / llama.cpp) — measured\n\n| Model | Q4_K_M | Q8_0 | f16/fp16 |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| TinyLlama-1.1B-Chat (TheBloke) | 669 MB | 1171 MB | — |\n| Llama-3.2-1B-Instruct (bartowski) | 808 MB | 1321 MB | 2480 MB |\n| Qwen2.5-1.5B-Instruct (Qwen) | 1117 MB | 1895 MB | — |\n| gemma-2-2b-it (bartowski) | 1709 MB | 2784 MB | — |\n| Phi-3-mini-4k-instruct (microsoft) | 2393 MB (`q4`) | — | 7643 MB (fp16) |\n\n### MLC (WebLLM) — measured\n\n| Model | Size (sum of shard files) |\n|---|---|\n| mlc-ai/Llama-3.2-1B-Instruct-q4f16_1-MLC | ~705 MB |\n\n**Rules of thumb for a device budget UI:** 135M–360M models fit comfortably anywhere (<300 MB at q8/q4); 0.5B–1B at q4 ≈ 0.5–1 GB; 1.5B–2B at q4 ≈ 1–1.7 GB; 3B at q4 ≈ 2 GB; Phi-3-mini (3.8B) at q4 ≈ 2.4 GB. WebGPU/`q4f16` ONNX variants are the smallest browser-friendly option.\n\n---\n\n## 6. Rate limits & auth for anonymous browser access\n\nFrom the official [Hub Rate limits](https://huggingface.co/docs/hub/en/rate-limits) page (tiers as of Sept '25), quotas are over **5-minute fixed windows**, applied **per IP** for anonymous users:\n\n| Plan | Hub **API** (`/api/*`) | **Resolvers** (`/resolve/*` downloads) | Pages |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| **Anonymous (per IP)** | **500** | **3,000** | 100 |\n| Free user (token) | 1,000 | 5,000 | 200 |\n| PRO | 2,500 | 12,000 | 400 |\n| Enterprise | 6,000 | 50,000 | 600 |\n\n- **No auth required** for public models — both `/api/models` and `/resolve/` work fully anonymously (all calls above used no token). Gated/private repos need a token.\n- Live headers confirm this: `ratelimit-policy: \"fixed window\";\"api\";q=500;w=300` and a live `ratelimit: \"api\";r=498;t=212` (remaining/seconds-to-reset) were returned on an anonymous API call.\n- On exceeding a quota you get **HTTP 429** with standardized `RateLimit` / `RateLimit-Policy` headers (IETF draft-9) carrying remaining count and reset seconds.\n- **Design guidance for a browser app:**\n  - **Resolver (download) limits (3,000/IP/5min) are ~6× the API limits and are infrastructure-optimized** — prefer downloads over heavy metadata polling. HF explicitly recommends replacing API calls with resolver calls where possible.\n  - Minimize `/api/models` calls (cache discovery results, paginate with `limit`); the 500/IP API budget is the tighter constraint, and is shared across all users behind a NAT/shared IP.\n  - Optionally let users supply an HF token to raise limits, but it is not required.\n\n---\n\n## Sources\n\n- Hub API Endpoints (now points to OpenAPI): https://huggingface.co/docs/hub/en/api\n- OpenAPI spec (JSON / Markdown): https://huggingface.co/.well-known/openapi.json — https://huggingface.co/.well-known/openapi.md\n- OpenAPI Playground: https://huggingface.co/spaces/huggingface/openapi\n- Hub Rate limits (tiers, 429, headers): https://huggingface.co/docs/hub/en/rate-limits\n- Search the Hub (filter/sort/limit, num_parameters, CLI): https://huggingface.co/docs/huggingface_hub/en/guides/search\n- HfApi client reference (list_models, expand keys): https://huggingface.co/docs/huggingface_hub/en/package_reference/hf_api\n- huggingface.js `@huggingface/hub` (listFiles/tree, parseSafetensorsMetadata, ListFileEntry/PathInfo): https://huggingface.co/docs/huggingface.js/hub/modules\n- transformers.js index (ONNX runtime, onnx-community vs Xenova): https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers.js/en/index\n- transformers.js library filter (UI): https://huggingface.co/models?library=transformers.js\n- transformers.js v3 (WebGPU): https://huggingface.co/blog/transformersjs-v3\n- transformers.js GGUF-support discussion (ONNX-only): https://github.com/huggingface/transformers.js/issues/734\n- Xet bridge CORS/Range (datasets context): https://github.com/huggingface/datasets/issues/7931\n- Live endpoints verified (anonymous, 2026-06-09): `https://huggingface.co/api/models?...`, `https://huggingface.co/api/models/{repo}/tree/main?recursive=true`, `https://huggingface.co/api/models/{repo}?expand[]=safetensors`, `https://huggingface.co/{repo}/resolve/main/{file}`\n\n### Key verified facts (quick reference)\n- `sort=downloads|likes|trendingScore` + `direction=-1` + `limit` all work anonymously; `full=true`/`config=true`/`expand[]=...` control field expansion.\n- Per-file sizes come from the **tree** endpoint (`size`, `lfs.size`); **`siblings` carry filenames only**. Sum `.onnx_data*` for sharded ONNX.\n- `expand[]=safetensors` → `{parameters:{<dtype>:N}, total:N}`.\n- `/resolve/` → 302 to `cas-bridge.xethub.hf.co`; CORS allow-origin echoed, Range/preflight succeed (206) — browser streaming works without a proxy.\n- Anonymous limits: **500 API / 3,000 resolver per IP per 5 min**; no token needed for public models; 429 + `RateLimit` headers on overflow."

[2026-06-09T22:52:17.307Z] [INFO]         "text": "I now have comprehensive, empirically-verified data. I have everything needed for the report. Let me compile the final structured markdown report.\n\n---\n\n# Hugging Face Hub API: Discovering & Downloading Small Models for Browser/Client-Side LLMs\n\nAll endpoint behaviors below were **verified empirically** with live `curl` calls against `huggingface.co` on 2026-06-09 (anonymous, no token), in addition to the official docs. The official Hub API reference has been migrated to an OpenAPI spec (`https://huggingface.co/.well-known/openapi.json` / `.md`) and an [OpenAPI Playground](https://huggingface.co/spaces/huggingface/openapi).\n\n## 1. `GET https://huggingface.co/api/models` — discovery & ranking\n\n### Query parameters (all confirmed working anonymously)\n\n| Param | Purpose | Example value |\n|---|---|---|\n| `search` | Free-text match on model id/name | `search=SmolLM2` |\n| `author` | Filter by namespace/org/user | `author=onnx-community` |\n| `filter` | Filter by **tag** (repeatable; AND-combined). Tags include library names, formats, languages, licenses | `filter=gguf`, `filter=transformers.js`, `filter=onnx` |\n| `pipeline_tag` | Filter by task | `pipeline_tag=text-generation` |\n| `library` | Filter by library | `library=transformers`, `library=transformers.js` |\n| `language` | Language code | `language=en` |\n| `sort` | Sort key: `downloads`, `likes`, `trendingScore`, `createdAt`, `lastModified` | `sort=downloads` |\n| `direction` | `-1` = descending, `1` = ascending | `direction=-1` |\n| `limit` | Page size (cap results) | `limit=20` |\n| `full` | `full=true` adds `tags`, `siblings`, `sha`, etc. to each entry | `full=true` |\n| `config` | `config=true` includes the model `config.json` summary | `config=true` |\n| `expand[]` | Selectively expand fields (alternative to `full`); e.g. `siblings`, `safetensors`, `gguf`, `downloads`, `likes`, `tags`, `pipeline_tag` | `expand[]=safetensors` |\n| `gated` | Filter gated repos | `gated=false` |\n\n> Note: `expand[]` and `full`/`config` are mutually exclusive in practice — use one style. With `expand[]`, only the requested fields (plus id) are returned.\n\n### Example URLs\n\n```\n# Top-downloaded SmolLM2 models, full metadata\nhttps://huggingface.co/api/models?search=SmolLM2&sort=downloads&direction=-1&limit=10&full=true\n\n# Trending text-generation models\nhttps://huggingface.co/api/models?pipeline_tag=text-generation&sort=trendingScore&direction=-1&limit=20\n\n# Browser-runnable (transformers.js) text-gen models by popularity\nhttps://huggingface.co/api/models?filter=transformers.js&pipeline_tag=text-generation&sort=downloads&limit=20\n\n# Most-liked ONNX exports from onnx-community\nhttps://huggingface.co/api/models?author=onnx-community&pipeline_tag=text-generation&sort=downloads&limit=20\n\n# All GGUF repos, most downloaded\nhttps://huggingface.co/api/models?filter=gguf&sort=downloads&limit=20\n```\n\n### Example response shape (one element, `sort=downloads`)\n\n```json\n{\n  \"id\": \"HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM2-135M-Instruct\",\n  \"author\": \"HuggingFaceTB\",\n  \"gated\": false,\n  \"likes\": 340,\n  \"downloads\": 1543897,\n  \"trendingScore\": 270,\n  \"private\": false,\n  \"tags\": [\"transformers\",\"onnx\",\"safetensors\",\"llama\",\"text-generation\",\n           \"transformers.js\",\"conversational\",\"en\",\"license:apache-2.0\", \"...\"],\n  \"pipeline_tag\": \"text-generation\",\n  \"library_name\": \"transformers\",\n  \"createdAt\": \"2024-10-31T13:41:10.000Z\",\n  \"siblings\": [{\"rfilename\": \"model.safetensors\"}, {\"rfilename\": \"onnx/model_q4.onnx\"}, \"...\"]\n}\n```\n\n**Pagination:** results page via the `Link` HTTP header (`rel=\"next\"`) and `X-Total-Count` (both are CORS-exposed — see §3).\n\n---\n\n## 2. Per-file sizes & parameter counts\n\n### Tree endpoint — the reliable way to get file sizes\n\n`GET https://huggingface.co/api/models/{repo}/tree/{revision}?recursive=true`\n\nParams: `recursive=true` (walk subdirs), `expand=true` (adds commit + security-scan data), `limit`, `cursor`. You can also scope to a subpath: `.../tree/main/onnx?recursive=true`.\n\nExample: `https://huggingface.co/api/models/HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM2-135M-Instruct/tree/main?recursive=true`\n\n```json\n[\n  {\"type\":\"directory\",\"oid\":\"703e76c4...\",\"size\":0,\"path\":\"onnx\"},\n  {\"type\":\"file\",\"oid\":\"2ef70737...\",\"size\":269060552,\n   \"lfs\":{\"oid\":\"5af571cb...\",\"size\":269060552,\"pointerSize\":134},\n   \"xetHash\":\"73b079dc...\",\"path\":\"model.safetensors\"},\n  {\"type\":\"file\",\"size\":137147867, \"lfs\":{\"size\":137147867,...},\n   \"path\":\"onnx/model_int8.onnx\"}\n]\n```\n\n- `size` is the **real byte size** (bytes). For LFS/Xet-backed files, `size` and `lfs.size` match the actual download size.\n- **Caveat:** sharded ONNX models store weights in external `model_xxx.onnx_data` files — the `.onnx` graph file then reports a tiny size (often `0`/few KB) and you must **sum the `.onnx` + its `.onnx_data*` siblings** to get true size (e.g. `onnx-community/Llama-3.2-1B-Instruct-ONNX`: `model_q4.onnx`≈0 MB + `model_q4.onnx_data`≈1693 MB).\n\n### `siblings` does NOT include sizes\n\nThe model-info `siblings` array (from `?full=true` or `?expand[]=siblings`) contains **only filenames**:\n```json\n\"siblings\": [{\"rfilename\": \".gitattributes\"}, {\"rfilename\": \"model.safetensors\"}]\n```\nSo for sizing you must use the **tree endpoint**, not `siblings`. (`?blobs=true` is a UI/blob-oriented flag; the tree endpoint's `size`/`lfs.size` is the dependable size source.)\n\n### Total parameter count — `?expand[]=safetensors`\n\n`GET https://huggingface.co/api/models/{repo}?expand[]=safetensors`\n\n```json\n{\n  \"id\": \"HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM2-135M-Instruct\",\n  \"safetensors\": {\n    \"parameters\": {\"BF16\": 134515008},\n    \"total\": 134515008\n  }\n}\n```\n`parameters` is a map of dtype → count; `total` is the grand total. You can request both `expand[]=safetensors&expand[]=gguf`. Under the hood the Hub derives this by range-reading the safetensors header / `model.safetensors.index.json` (the `huggingface.js` `parseSafetensorsMetadata` does the same, returning `parameterCount` and `parameterTotal`).\n\n---\n\n## 3. Download (resolve) URL pattern + CORS\n\n### Pattern\n```\nhttps://huggingface.co/{repo}/resolve/{revision}/{path}\ne.g. https://huggingface.co/HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM2-135M-Instruct/resolve/main/onnx/model_q4.onnx\n```\n`{revision}` = branch (`main`), tag, or commit SHA. Use a commit SHA for cache-immutable URLs.\n\n### CORS behavior (verified — browser fetch friendly)\n- A `GET` to `/resolve/...` returns **HTTP 302** redirecting to the Xet CDN (`https://cas-bridge.xethub.hf.co/...`) with a signed, time-limited URL. `fetch()` follows this transparently.\n- The huggingface.co response includes `access-control-allow-origin: <echoes your Origin>` and `access-control-expose-headers: ... Content-Range, ETag, X-Linked-Size, X-Linked-ETag, X-Xet-Hash, Accept-Ranges, ...`. `X-Linked-Size` gives the file size on the redirect response.\n- **Range requests work cross-origin.** A `Range: bytes=0-1023` request returns **206 Partial Content** from the CDN with `access-control-allow-origin`, `access-control-allow-credentials: true`, and exposed `content-range`/`accept-ranges`. \n- **CORS preflight (OPTIONS) succeeds:** `OPTIONS` with `Access-Control-Request-Headers: range` → **200** with `access-control-allow-methods: GET`, `access-control-allow-headers: range`, `access-control-max-age: 86400`.\n\n> This means streaming/chunked/resumable downloads from the browser (as transformers.js, WebLLM, and wllama do) work without a proxy. (Historical note: some earlier GitHub issues reported Range/preflight CORS failures on the Xet bridge for *dataset* Parquet partial reads — but model `/resolve/` Range fetches are working as tested here.)\n\n---\n\n## 4. Filtering for browser-runnable formats\n\n| Runtime | What to filter | Orgs / tags |\n|---|---|---|\n| **transformers.js** (ONNX Runtime Web, WebGPU) | `filter=transformers.js` or `library=transformers.js`; tag `onnx` | **onnx-community** (recommended, current exports), **Xenova** (legacy v2 ONNX). UI filter: `https://huggingface.co/models?library=transformers.js` |\n| **WebLLM / MLC** | `author=mlc-ai`; repos suffixed with quant scheme e.g. `-q4f16_1-MLC`, `-q4f32_1-MLC` | **mlc-ai** org (prebuilt WASM/WebGPU libraries). Also `mlc-llm` tag |\n| **wllama / llama.cpp-wasm** | `filter=gguf`; tag `gguf` | `bartowski`, `TheBloke`, `Qwen`, `microsoft`, `unsloth`, etc. |\n\nUseful task/format tags: `text-generation` (pipeline_tag), `transformers.js`, `onnx`, `gguf`, `conversational`, `mlc-llm`. ONNX repos expose multiple precisions as separate files in `onnx/`: `model.onnx` (fp32), `model_fp16.onnx`, `model_int8.onnx`/`model_quantized.onnx`/`model_uint8.onnx`, `model_q4.onnx`, `model_q4f16.onnx`, `model_bnb4.onnx` — letting the app pick a precision by device budget.\n\n> Note: transformers.js currently runs **ONNX only** (no GGUF). GGUF in-browser needs wllama/llama.cpp-wasm; MLC format needs WebLLM.\n\n---\n\n## 5. Quantization → size (verified file sizes, MB ≈ size/1e6)\n\nONNX dtypes: `fp16` ≈ 2 bytes/param, `int8`/`q8`/`uint8` ≈ 1 byte/param, `q4`/`q4f16` ≈ 0.5–0.6 byte/param. GGUF: `Q4_K_M` ≈ 4.5 bits/param, `Q8_0` ≈ 8.5 bits/param, `f16` ≈ 16 bits/param.\n\n### ONNX (transformers.js) — measured\n\n| Model | fp16 | int8/quantized | q4 | q4f16 |\n|---|---|---|---|---|\n| SmolLM2-135M-Instruct | 270 MB | 137 MB | — | — (fp32 `model.onnx` = 540 MB; safetensors = 269 MB) |\n| SmolLM2-360M-Instruct | — | — | — | — (fp32 = 1450 MB; safetensors = 724 MB) |\n| onnx-community/Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct | 997 MB | 512 MB | 786 MB | **483 MB** |\n| onnx-community/Llama-3.2-1B-Instruct (sharded, sum `.onnx`+`.onnx_data`) | ~2488 MB | 1237 MB | ~1693 MB | ~1090 MB |\n\n### GGUF (wllama / llama.cpp) — measured\n\n| Model | Q4_K_M | Q8_0 | f16/fp16 |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| TinyLlama-1.1B-Chat (TheBloke) | 669 MB | 1171 MB | — |\n| Llama-3.2-1B-Instruct (bartowski) | 808 MB | 1321 MB | 2480 MB |\n| Qwen2.5-1.5B-Instruct (Qwen) | 1117 MB | 1895 MB | — |\n| gemma-2-2b-it (bartowski) | 1709 MB | 2784 MB | — |\n| Phi-3-mini-4k-instruct (microsoft) | 2393 MB (`q4`) | — | 7643 MB (fp16) |\n\n### MLC (WebLLM) — measured\n\n| Model | Size (sum of shard files) |\n|---|---|\n| mlc-ai/Llama-3.2-1B-Instruct-q4f16_1-MLC | ~705 MB |\n\n**Rules of thumb for a device budget UI:** 135M–360M models fit comfortably anywhere (<300 MB at q8/q4); 0.5B–1B at q4 ≈ 0.5–1 GB; 1.5B–2B at q4 ≈ 1–1.7 GB; 3B at q4 ≈ 2 GB; Phi-3-mini (3.8B) at q4 ≈ 2.4 GB. WebGPU/`q4f16` ONNX variants are the smallest browser-friendly option.\n\n---\n\n## 6. Rate limits & auth for anonymous browser access\n\nFrom the official [Hub Rate limits](https://huggingface.co/docs/hub/en/rate-limits) page (tiers as of Sept '25), quotas are over **5-minute fixed windows**, applied **per IP** for anonymous users:\n\n| Plan | Hub **API** (`/api/*`) | **Resolvers** (`/resolve/*` downloads) | Pages |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| **Anonymous (per IP)** | **500** | **3,000** | 100 |\n| Free user (token) | 1,000 | 5,000 | 200 |\n| PRO | 2,500 | 12,000 | 400 |\n| Enterprise | 6,000 | 50,000 | 600 |\n\n- **No auth required** for public models — both `/api/models` and `/resolve/` work fully anonymously (all calls above used no token). Gated/private repos need a token.\n- Live headers confirm this: `ratelimit-policy: \"fixed window\";\"api\";q=500;w=300` and a live `ratelimit: \"api\";r=498;t=212` (remaining/seconds-to-reset) were returned on an anonymous API call.\n- On exceeding a quota you get **HTTP 429** with standardized `RateLimit` / `RateLimit-Policy` headers (IETF draft-9) carrying remaining count and reset seconds.\n- **Design guidance for a browser app:**\n  - **Resolver (download) limits (3,000/IP/5min) are ~6× the API limits and are infrastructure-optimized** — prefer downloads over heavy metadata polling. HF explicitly recommends replacing API calls with resolver calls where possible.\n  - Minimize `/api/models` calls (cache discovery results, paginate with `limit`); the 500/IP API budget is the tighter constraint, and is shared across all users behind a NAT/shared IP.\n  - Optionally let users supply an HF token to raise limits, but it is not required.\n\n---\n\n## Sources\n\n- Hub API Endpoints (now points to OpenAPI): https://huggingface.co/docs/hub/en/api\n- OpenAPI spec (JSON / Markdown): https://huggingface.co/.well-known/openapi.json — https://huggingface.co/.well-known/openapi.md\n- OpenAPI Playground: https://huggingface.co/spaces/huggingface/openapi\n- Hub Rate limits (tiers, 429, headers): https://huggingface.co/docs/hub/en/rate-limits\n- Search the Hub (filter/sort/limit, num_parameters, CLI): https://huggingface.co/docs/huggingface_hub/en/guides/search\n- HfApi client reference (list_models, expand keys): https://huggingface.co/docs/huggingface_hub/en/package_reference/hf_api\n- huggingface.js `@huggingface/hub` (listFiles/tree, parseSafetensorsMetadata, ListFileEntry/PathInfo): https://huggingface.co/docs/huggingface.js/hub/modules\n- transformers.js index (ONNX runtime, onnx-community vs Xenova): https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers.js/en/index\n- transformers.js library filter (UI): https://huggingface.co/models?library=transformers.js\n- transformers.js v3 (WebGPU): https://huggingface.co/blog/transformersjs-v3\n- transformers.js GGUF-support discussion (ONNX-only): https://github.com/huggingface/transformers.js/issues/734\n- Xet bridge CORS/Range (datasets context): https://github.com/huggingface/datasets/issues/7931\n- Live endpoints verified (anonymous, 2026-06-09): `https://huggingface.co/api/models?...`, `https://huggingface.co/api/models/{repo}/tree/main?recursive=true`, `https://huggingface.co/api/models/{repo}?expand[]=safetensors`, `https://huggingface.co/{repo}/resolve/main/{file}`\n\n### Key verified facts (quick reference)\n- `sort=downloads|likes|trendingScore` + `direction=-1` + `limit` all work anonymously; `full=true`/`config=true`/`expand[]=...` control field expansion.\n- Per-file sizes come from the **tree** endpoint (`size`, `lfs.size`); **`siblings` carry filenames only**. Sum `.onnx_data*` for sharded ONNX.\n- `expand[]=safetensors` → `{parameters:{<dtype>:N}, total:N}`.\n- `/resolve/` → 302 to `cas-bridge.xethub.hf.co`; CORS allow-origin echoed, Range/preflight succeed (206) — browser streaming works without a proxy.\n- Anonymous limits: **500 API / 3,000 resolver per IP per 5 min**; no token needed for public models; 429 + `RateLimit` headers on overflow."

[2026-06-09T22:52:19.823Z] [INFO]             "text": "I have all the data I need, well-corroborated across primary sources. Note one correction: wllama added experimental WebGPU support in v3.1 (it's no longer purely CPU). Candle's WASM is CPU-only (no WebGPU in the wasm browser target). Let me compile the final report.\n\n---\n\n# Browser-Side Small Language Model (SLM) Inference Libraries — Research Report\n\n**Date:** June 2026 · **Context:** Project currently runs SmolLM2-135M via Rust + `candle` compiled to WASM. Goal: dynamically pick and download the most popular model that fits the user's device, fully client-side, with on-demand download + caching.\n\n## TL;DR Recommendation\n\n**WebLLM (`@mlc-ai/web-llm`)** is the best fit for \"dynamically pick and download the most popular model that fits the device.\" It is the only library that ships a **machine-readable catalog** (`prebuiltAppConfig.model_list`) with **per-model `vram_required_MB`** values, plus OpenAI-compatible streaming and robust caching (Cache API / IndexedDB / OPFS). You query the device's available VRAM, filter the catalog by `vram_required_MB`, pick the largest/most-popular model that fits, and load it by `model_id`.\n\nFor a **WASM/CPU fallback** (no WebGPU — Firefox/Safari/old GPUs), pair WebLLM with **wllama** (`@wllama/wllama`, GGUF) or **transformers.js** (`@huggingface/transformers`, ONNX, which auto-falls back WASM↔WebGPU). The strongest production setup is a **two-tier strategy: WebLLM when WebGPU is available, transformers.js or wllama otherwise.**\n\n---\n\n## Comparison Table\n\n| Library | GitHub URL | Stars (~Jun 2026) | License | Backend | Model format | On-demand DL + cache | Streaming | npm package | Maturity |\n|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|\n| **transformers.js** | github.com/huggingface/transformers.js | ~16.1k | Apache-2.0 | WASM **+** WebGPU (auto) | ONNX | HF Hub download + browser **Cache API** | Yes (`TextStreamer`) | `@huggingface/transformers` (v4.x) | High — 170+ architectures, 1,200+ models |\n| **WebLLM** | github.com/mlc-ai/web-llm | ~18.2k | Apache-2.0 | **WebGPU only** | MLC-compiled (`.wasm` lib + weights) | `prebuiltAppConfig` catalog; **Cache API / IndexedDB / OPFS** | Yes (OpenAI-compatible) | `@mlc-ai/web-llm` (v0.2.8x) | High — production, OpenAI API |\n| **wllama** | github.com/ngxson/wllama | ~1.1k | MIT | **WASM/CPU** (+ experimental WebGPU since v3.1) | **GGUF** | URL/HF download, **split-model** parallel chunks, ModelManager cache | Yes (token callback) | `@wllama/wllama` (v3.4.x) | Medium — active, smaller community |\n| **candle (wasm)** | github.com/huggingface/candle | ~20.4k | Apache-2.0 / MIT | **WASM/CPU only** (no WebGPU in wasm target) | safetensors/GGUF (Rust) | Per-example fetch; **no turnkey cache** | Example-dependent | none (Rust framework + demo crates) | Framework, not a JS lib — **current approach** |\n| **ratchet** | github.com/huggingface/ratchet | ~760 | MIT | **WebGPU** (+ CPU) | custom (GGUF-derived) | IndexedDB cache | Not documented | none published | **Experimental** — \"active development\" |\n| **picoLLM** | github.com/Picovoice/picollm | ~310 | Apache-2.0 (SDK) | **WASM** (cross-browser) | proprietary `.pllm` (X-bit quant) | Console download; **requires AccessKey + license-server validation** | Yes (`generate`/`interrupt`) | `@picovoice/picollm-web` | Medium — **commercial, gated** |\n| **MediaPipe LLM Inference** | github.com/google-ai-edge/mediapipe | (part of mediapipe) | Apache-2.0 (Prohibited-Use policy) | **WebGPU** | `.task` / `.litertlm` / `.bin` | `modelAssetPath`; **no built-in catalog/cache** | Yes (callback) | `@mediapipe/tasks-genai` | Medium — Google-backed, Gemma-focused |\n| **Chrome Prompt API** | developer.chrome.com/docs/ai | n/a (browser API) | Chrome | built-in (Gemini Nano) | fixed (Gemini Nano) | Browser-managed download | Yes | none (`LanguageModel` global) | **Stable Chrome 138+, desktop only** |\n\n---\n\n## Library Notes\n\n### 1. transformers.js (`@huggingface/transformers`)\n- ONNX Runtime Web; **defaults to WASM/CPU, opt into GPU with `device: 'webgpu'`** (claimed up to ~100x faster than WASM). v4 ships a rewritten C++ WebGPU runtime.\n- Models pulled from the HF Hub orgs **`onnx-community`** and **`Xenova`**; flexible quantization via `dtype` (`fp32/fp16/q8/q4`). Model files cached automatically in the **browser Cache API** (works offline after first load).\n- `pipeline()` API; streaming via `TextStreamer`. Best **cross-browser** option because it auto-falls back to WASM where WebGPU is unavailable. Has SmolLM2, Qwen2.5, Llama-3.2 ONNX builds on the Hub.\n\n### 2. WebLLM (`@mlc-ai/web-llm`) — recommended\n- **WebGPU only.** Models are pre-compiled to MLC format (a WebGPU WASM `model_lib` + quantized weights). Quantization codes: `q4f16_1` (recommended), `q4f32_1`, `q0f16/q0f32`.\n- The killer feature: `webllm.prebuiltAppConfig.model_list` is a registry where **every entry carries `vram_required_MB`** — exactly what device-fit selection needs (see catalog below). Load via `CreateMLCEngine(model_id)`.\n- Caching: Cache API (default), IndexedDB, OPFS, plus experimental cross-origin storage. **Fully OpenAI-compatible** (streaming, JSON mode, function calling). Runs in Web Worker / Service Worker.\n\n### 3. wllama (`@wllama/wllama`)\n- llama.cpp compiled to WASM; **GGUF** directly from HF/URLs. Primarily **CPU/SIMD multi-thread** (needs COOP/COEP cross-origin-isolation headers for threads); **experimental WebGPU since v3.1**.\n- **Split-model download** (`llama-gguf-split`, recommended ≤512 MB chunks, parallel downloads) works around the **2 GB `ArrayBuffer` limit** and speeds downloads. `ModelManager` lists/validates/caches models. Runs in a Web Worker. Best **CPU fallback** path and the natural GGUF analog to your current candle setup.\n\n### 4. candle wasm (current approach)\n- `candle` is a **Rust ML framework**, not a JS library. The `candle-wasm-examples` crates (llama2-c, phi, quant-qwen3, whisper, bert, etc.) are **demo apps**, **CPU/WASM only — no WebGPU in the wasm browser target**, and have **no turnkey download/caching/catalog layer** (you build that yourself, as you have). This is why migrating to WebLLM/transformers.js buys you WebGPU + caching + a model catalog \"for free.\"\n\n### 5. Secondary / constrained options\n- **ratchet** (HF) — WebGPU, Rust→WASM, IndexedDB caching; supports Whisper, Phi-2/3, Moondream. **Experimental**, no published npm package, small model coverage. Not yet production-ready.\n- **picoLLM** (Picovoice) — cross-browser WASM, novel X-bit quantization, 30+ models. **Gated by an AccessKey requiring online license-server validation** — disqualifying for a fully-open, no-key client-side goal.\n- **MediaPipe LLM Inference** (`@mediapipe/tasks-genai`) — WebGPU, `.task`/`.litertlm`, Gemma-centric (Gemma-2/3/3n multimodal), Phi-2, Falcon, StableLM. Streaming yes, but **no built-in catalog or device-fit logic** and subject to Google's Prohibited-Use policy.\n- **Chrome Prompt API / Gemini Nano** (`LanguageModel` global) — **stable in Chrome 138+** but **desktop only** (no Android/iOS), needs **~22 GB free disk**, 16 GB RAM or >4 GB VRAM, and you **cannot choose the model**. Great as a zero-download fast path when present, useless as the primary cross-platform engine.\n\n---\n\n## Small-Model Catalog (browser-suitable, quantized)\n\nVRAM figures are WebLLM `vram_required_MB` (verbatim from `prebuiltAppConfig`); \"—\" means not in WebLLM's catalog (still runnable via transformers.js/wllama). WASM/CPU RAM needs are roughly similar to or a bit higher than the listed VRAM.\n\n| Model | Best quant | VRAM (WebLLM) | WebGPU | WASM/CPU | Notes |\n|---|---|---|---|---|---|\n| **SmolLM2-135M** | q0f16 | **~360 MB** | ✅ | ✅ (your current) | Tiniest; instant load |\n| **SmolLM2-360M** | q4f16_1 | **~376 MB** | ✅ | ✅ | Great default tiny tier |\n| **SmolLM2-1.7B** | q4f16_1 | **~1,774 MB** | ✅ | ✅ (slow) | |\n| **Qwen2.5-0.5B** | q4f16_1 | **~945 MB** | ✅ | ✅ | |\n| **Qwen2.5-1.5B** | q4f16_1 | **~1,630 MB** | ✅ | ✅ | Strong quality/size |\n| **Qwen2.5-3B** | q4f16_1 | **~2,505 MB** | ✅ | △ (heavy) | |\n| **Llama-3.2-1B** | q4f16_1 | **~879 MB** | ✅ | ✅ | Popular 1B baseline |\n| **Llama-3.2-3B** | q4f16_1 | **~2,264 MB** | ✅ | △ | |\n| **Gemma-2-2b-it** | q4f16_1 | **~1,895 MB** (4k) | ✅ | △ | 1k-ctx variant ~1,583 MB |\n| **Phi-3.5-mini** | q4f16_1 | **~3,672 MB** (4k) | ✅ (needs ≥4 GB) | ✗ practical | 1k-ctx variant ~2,520 MB |\n| **TinyLlama-1.1B** | q4f16_1 | **~697 MB** | ✅ | ✅ | Very light |\n| **Qwen3-0.6B** | q4f16_1 | **~1,403 MB** | ✅ | ✅ | Newer, reasoning-capable |\n| **H2O Danube (Danube2/3 ~1.8B)** | q4 GGUF | — | ✅ (wllama/tjs) | ✅ | Not in WebLLM catalog; use GGUF via wllama |\n\n**Device tiers (rule of thumb):**\n- **≤1 GB VRAM / CPU-only:** SmolLM2-135M/360M, TinyLlama-1.1B, Qwen2.5-0.5B, Llama-3.2-1B (q4).\n- **~2–3 GB VRAM:** Qwen2.5-1.5B, Gemma-2-2b, SmolLM2-1.7B, Qwen3-0.6B, Llama-3.2-3B, Qwen2.5-3B.\n- **≥4 GB VRAM:** Phi-3.5-mini and above.\n\n---\n\n## Recommended Architecture for \"pick + download best-fitting model\"\n\n1. **Detect capability:** check `navigator.gpu` (WebGPU present?) and request an adapter to estimate `maxBufferSize` / limits; fall back to `navigator.deviceMemory` for a RAM hint.\n2. **If WebGPU available → WebLLM.** Filter `prebuiltAppConfig.model_list` by `vram_required_MB ≤ budget`, rank by your \"popularity\" preference (e.g., Llama-3.2 / Qwen2.5 / SmolLM2 families), and `CreateMLCEngine(model_id)`. Caching and streaming come built-in.\n3. **If no WebGPU → transformers.js (`device:'wasm'`)** or **wllama (GGUF, split download)** with a small model (SmolLM2-360M / Qwen2.5-0.5B / Llama-3.2-1B q4).\n4. **Optional fast path:** if `LanguageModel`/Prompt API is available (Chrome 138+ desktop), offer Gemini Nano with zero download.\n5. **Keep candle** only if you need a single tiny CPU model with no dependencies — but it gives you no WebGPU, no catalog, and no caching layer, all of which WebLLM/transformers.js provide out of the box.\n\n---\n\n## Sources\n\n- Transformers.js — GitHub: https://github.com/huggingface/transformers.js\n- Transformers.js — docs: https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers.js/index\n- Transformers.js v3 (WebGPU) blog: VRAM): https://github.com/mlc-ai/web-llm/blob/main/src/config.ts (raw: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mlc-ai/web-llm/main/src/config.ts)\n- WebLLM — npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/@mlc-ai/web-llm\n- WebLLM — MLC deploy docs: https://llm.mlc.ai/docs/deploy/webllm.html\n- wllama — GitHub: https://github.com/ngxson/wllama\n- wllama — README: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ngxson/wllama/master/README.md\n- wllama — docs: https://github.ngxson.com/wllama/docs/\n- wllama — npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/@wllama/wllama\n- candle — caveats / corrections to the brief:** wllama is no longer strictly CPU-only — it added **experimental WebGPU in v3.1**, though WASM/CPU is still its primary, most-reliable path. candle's **wasm browser target is CPU-only** (no WebGPU). picoLLM **requires an AccessKey validated against Picovoice's license servers**, which conflicts with a fully self-contained client-side goal. Star counts are approximate live GitHub values read in June 2026."

[2026-06-09T22:52:19.823Z] [INFO]         "text": "I have all the data I need, well-corroborated across primary sources. Note one correction: wllama added experimental WebGPU support in v3.1 (it's no longer purely CPU). Candle's WASM is CPU-only (no WebGPU in the wasm browser target). Let me compile the final report.\n\n---\n\n# Browser-Side Small Language Model (SLM) Inference Libraries — Research Report\n\n**Date:** June 2026 · **Context:** Project currently runs SmolLM2-135M via Rust + `candle` compiled to WASM. Goal: dynamically pick and download the most popular model that fits the user's device, fully client-side, with on-demand download + caching.\n\n## TL;DR Recommendation\n\n**WebLLM (`@mlc-ai/web-llm`)** is the best fit for \"dynamically pick and download the most popular model that fits the device.\" It is the only library that ships a **machine-readable catalog** (`prebuiltAppConfig.model_list`) with **per-model `vram_required_MB`** values, plus OpenAI-compatible streaming and robust caching (Cache API / IndexedDB / OPFS). You query the device's available VRAM, filter the catalog by `vram_required_MB`, pick the largest/most-popular model that fits, and load it by `model_id`.\n\nFor a **WASM/CPU fallback** (no WebGPU — Firefox/Safari/old GPUs), pair WebLLM with **wllama** (`@wllama/wllama`, GGUF) or **transformers.js** (`@huggingface/transformers`, ONNX, which auto-falls back WASM↔WebGPU). The strongest production setup is a **two-tier strategy: WebLLM when WebGPU is available, transformers.js or wllama otherwise.**\n\n---\n\n## Comparison Table\n\n| Library | GitHub URL | Stars (~Jun 2026) | License | Backend | Model format | On-demand DL + cache | Streaming | npm package | Maturity |\n|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|\n| **transformers.js** | github.com/huggingface/transformers.js | ~16.1k | Apache-2.0 | WASM **+** WebGPU (auto) | ONNX | HF Hub download + browser **Cache API** | Yes (`TextStreamer`) | `@huggingface/transformers` (v4.x) | High — 170+ architectures, 1,200+ models |\n| **WebLLM** | github.com/mlc-ai/web-llm | ~18.2k | Apache-2.0 | **WebGPU only** | MLC-compiled (`.wasm` lib + weights) | `prebuiltAppConfig` catalog; **Cache API / IndexedDB / OPFS** | Yes (OpenAI-compatible) | `@mlc-ai/web-llm` (v0.2.8x) | High — production, OpenAI API |\n| **wllama** | github.com/ngxson/wllama | ~1.1k | MIT | **WASM/CPU** (+ experimental WebGPU since v3.1) | **GGUF** | URL/HF download, **split-model** parallel chunks, ModelManager cache | Yes (token callback) | `@wllama/wllama` (v3.4.x) | Medium — active, smaller community |\n| **candle (wasm)** | github.com/huggingface/candle | ~20.4k | Apache-2.0 / MIT | **WASM/CPU only** (no WebGPU in wasm target) | safetensors/GGUF (Rust) | Per-example fetch; **no turnkey cache** | Example-dependent | none (Rust framework + demo crates) | Framework, not a JS lib — **current approach** |\n| **ratchet** | github.com/huggingface/ratchet | ~760 | MIT | **WebGPU** (+ CPU) | custom (GGUF-derived) | IndexedDB cache | Not documented | none published | **Experimental** — \"active development\" |\n| **picoLLM** | github.com/Picovoice/picollm | ~310 | Apache-2.0 (SDK) | **WASM** (cross-browser) | proprietary `.pllm` (X-bit quant) | Console download; **requires AccessKey + license-server validation** | Yes (`generate`/`interrupt`) | `@picovoice/picollm-web` | Medium — **commercial, gated** |\n| **MediaPipe LLM Inference** | github.com/google-ai-edge/mediapipe | (part of mediapipe) | Apache-2.0 (Prohibited-Use policy) | **WebGPU** | `.task` / `.litertlm` / `.bin` | `modelAssetPath`; **no built-in catalog/cache** | Yes (callback) | `@mediapipe/tasks-genai` | Medium — Google-backed, Gemma-focused |\n| **Chrome Prompt API** | developer.chrome.com/docs/ai | n/a (browser API) | Chrome | built-in (Gemini Nano) | fixed (Gemini Nano) | Browser-managed download | Yes | none (`LanguageModel` global) | **Stable Chrome 138+, desktop only** |\n\n---\n\n## Library Notes\n\n### 1. transformers.js (`@huggingface/transformers`)\n- ONNX Runtime Web; **defaults to WASM/CPU, opt into GPU with `device: 'webgpu'`** (claimed up to ~100x faster than WASM). v4 ships a rewritten C++ WebGPU runtime.\n- Models pulled from the HF Hub orgs **`onnx-community`** and **`Xenova`**; flexible quantization via `dtype` (`fp32/fp16/q8/q4`). Model files cached automatically in the **browser Cache API** (works offline after first load).\n- `pipeline()` API; streaming via `TextStreamer`. Best **cross-browser** option because it auto-falls back to WASM where WebGPU is unavailable. Has SmolLM2, Qwen2.5, Llama-3.2 ONNX builds on the Hub.\n\n### 2. WebLLM (`@mlc-ai/web-llm`) — recommended\n- **WebGPU only.** Models are pre-compiled to MLC format (a WebGPU WASM `model_lib` + quantized weights). Quantization codes: `q4f16_1` (recommended), `q4f32_1`, `q0f16/q0f32`.\n- The killer feature: `webllm.prebuiltAppConfig.model_list` is a registry where **every entry carries `vram_required_MB`** — exactly what device-fit selection needs (see catalog below). Load via `CreateMLCEngine(model_id)`.\n- Caching: Cache API (default), IndexedDB, OPFS, plus experimental cross-origin storage. **Fully OpenAI-compatible** (streaming, JSON mode, function calling). Runs in Web Worker / Service Worker.\n\n### 3. wllama (`@wllama/wllama`)\n- llama.cpp compiled to WASM; **GGUF** directly from HF/URLs. Primarily **CPU/SIMD multi-thread** (needs COOP/COEP cross-origin-isolation headers for threads); **experimental WebGPU since v3.1**.\n- **Split-model download** (`llama-gguf-split`, recommended ≤512 MB chunks, parallel downloads) works around the **2 GB `ArrayBuffer` limit** and speeds downloads. `ModelManager` lists/validates/caches models. Runs in a Web Worker. Best **CPU fallback** path and the natural GGUF analog to your current candle setup.\n\n### 4. candle wasm (current approach)\n- `candle` is a **Rust ML framework**, not a JS library. The `candle-wasm-examples` crates (llama2-c, phi, quant-qwen3, whisper, bert, etc.) are **demo apps**, **CPU/WASM only — no WebGPU in the wasm browser target**, and have **no turnkey download/caching/catalog layer** (you build that yourself, as you have). This is why migrating to WebLLM/transformers.js buys you WebGPU + caching + a model catalog \"for free.\"\n\n### 5. Secondary / constrained options\n- **ratchet** (HF) — WebGPU, Rust→WASM, IndexedDB caching; supports Whisper, Phi-2/3, Moondream. **Experimental**, no published npm package, small model coverage. Not yet production-ready.\n- **picoLLM** (Picovoice) — cross-browser WASM, novel X-bit quantization, 30+ models. **Gated by an AccessKey requiring online license-server validation** — disqualifying for a fully-open, no-key client-side goal.\n- **MediaPipe LLM Inference** (`@mediapipe/tasks-genai`) — WebGPU, `.task`/`.litertlm`, Gemma-centric (Gemma-2/3/3n multimodal), Phi-2, Falcon, StableLM. Streaming yes, but **no built-in catalog or device-fit logic** and subject to Google's Prohibited-Use policy.\n- **Chrome Prompt API / Gemini Nano** (`LanguageModel` global) — **stable in Chrome 138+** but **desktop only** (no Android/iOS), needs **~22 GB free disk**, 16 GB RAM or >4 GB VRAM, and you **cannot choose the model**. Great as a zero-download fast path when present, useless as the primary cross-platform engine.\n\n---\n\n## Small-Model Catalog (browser-suitable, quantized)\n\nVRAM figures are WebLLM `vram_required_MB` (verbatim from `prebuiltAppConfig`); \"—\" means not in WebLLM's catalog (still runnable via transformers.js/wllama). WASM/CPU RAM needs are roughly similar to or a bit higher than the listed VRAM.\n\n| Model | Best quant | VRAM (WebLLM) | WebGPU | WASM/CPU | Notes |\n|---|---|---|---|---|---|\n| **SmolLM2-135M** | q0f16 | **~360 MB** | ✅ | ✅ (your current) | Tiniest; instant load |\n| **SmolLM2-360M** | q4f16_1 | **~376 MB** | ✅ | ✅ | Great default tiny tier |\n| **SmolLM2-1.7B** | q4f16_1 | **~1,774 MB** | ✅ | ✅ (slow) | |\n| **Qwen2.5-0.5B** | q4f16_1 | **~945 MB** | ✅ | ✅ | |\n| **Qwen2.5-1.5B** | q4f16_1 | **~1,630 MB** | ✅ | ✅ | Strong quality/size |\n| **Qwen2.5-3B** | q4f16_1 | **~2,505 MB** | ✅ | △ (heavy) | |\n| **Llama-3.2-1B** | q4f16_1 | **~879 MB** | ✅ | ✅ | Popular 1B baseline |\n| **Llama-3.2-3B** | q4f16_1 | **~2,264 MB** | ✅ | △ | |\n| **Gemma-2-2b-it** | q4f16_1 | **~1,895 MB** (4k) | ✅ | △ | 1k-ctx variant ~1,583 MB |\n| **Phi-3.5-mini** | q4f16_1 | **~3,672 MB** (4k) | ✅ (needs ≥4 GB) | ✗ practical | 1k-ctx variant ~2,520 MB |\n| **TinyLlama-1.1B** | q4f16_1 | **~697 MB** | ✅ | ✅ | Very light |\n| **Qwen3-0.6B** | q4f16_1 | **~1,403 MB** | ✅ | ✅ | Newer, reasoning-capable |\n| **H2O Danube (Danube2/3 ~1.8B)** | q4 GGUF | — | ✅ (wllama/tjs) | ✅ | Not in WebLLM catalog; use GGUF via wllama |\n\n**Device tiers (rule of thumb):**\n- **≤1 GB VRAM / CPU-only:** SmolLM2-135M/360M, TinyLlama-1.1B, Qwen2.5-0.5B, Llama-3.2-1B (q4).\n- **~2–3 GB VRAM:** Qwen2.5-1.5B, Gemma-2-2b, SmolLM2-1.7B, Qwen3-0.6B, Llama-3.2-3B, Qwen2.5-3B.\n- **≥4 GB VRAM:** Phi-3.5-mini and above.\n\n---\n\n## Recommended Architecture for \"pick + download best-fitting model\"\n\n1. **Detect capability:** check `navigator.gpu` (WebGPU present?) and request an adapter to estimate `maxBufferSize` / limits; fall back to `navigator.deviceMemory` for a RAM hint.\n2. **If WebGPU available → WebLLM.** Filter `prebuiltAppConfig.model_list` by `vram_required_MB ≤ budget`, rank by your \"popularity\" preference (e.g., Llama-3.2 / Qwen2.5 / SmolLM2 families), and `CreateMLCEngine(model_id)`. Caching and streaming come built-in.\n3. **If no WebGPU → transformers.js (`device:'wasm'`)** or **wllama (GGUF, split download)** with a small model (SmolLM2-360M / Qwen2.5-0.5B / Llama-3.2-1B q4).\n4. **Optional fast path:** if `LanguageModel`/Prompt API is available (Chrome 138+ desktop), offer Gemini Nano with zero download.\n5. **Keep candle** only if you need a single tiny CPU model with no dependencies — but it gives you no WebGPU, no catalog, and no caching layer, all of which WebLLM/transformers.js provide out of the box.\n\n---\n\n## Sources\n\n- Transformers.js — GitHub:
